sections.heading: Well Water Testing in Amherst, MA
sections.meta: Standalone: $225–$425 · As add-on: Bundle with home inspection — package pricing · 5–10 business days from sample collection

sections.heading: What Amherst Buyers and Owners Need to Know

blocks.html: Amherst has town water in most of the developed core, but North Amherst, Pelham-side East Amherst, and outlying parcels run on private wells drawing from fractured-bedrock aquifers. Bedrock wells in this part of Hampshire County routinely show elevated arsenic, uranium, and radon-in-water — we test the full panel, not just bacteria, because the dissolved-mineral risks here are real.

Ken Arnold has been performing well water testing services across the Pioneer Valley since 2004 — including hundreds of Amherst-area properties. Every inspection is performed personally by Ken (MA License #587), follows ASHI standards, and is delivered as a comprehensive photo-documented digital report.
Comprehensive pre-purchase well water testing for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Lab analysis through state-certified labs, results in 5–10 business days.
For full scope, methodology, and pricing details, see the canonical service page: Well Water Testing. For broader context on Amherst's housing stock, see the Amherst town profile.

items.a: Standard Massachusetts pre-purchase panels cover: total coliform and E. coli bacteria, nitrate and nitrite, hardness, pH, iron, manganese, sodium, chloride, sulfate, and lead. Many lenders also require arsenic, uranium, and radon-in-water for properties in geologically active areas. Specialty panels are available for VOCs, pesticides, PFAS, and other contaminants of concern.

items.q: Why is well water testing different in Western Mass?
items.a: The Pioneer Valley's geology — particularly the granite and pegmatite formations across Franklin County and parts of Hampshire County — produces elevated levels of naturally occurring contaminants. Iron and manganese are extremely common; arsenic occurs in pockets across the region; uranium and radon-in-water are documented in specific geological zones. A standard 'safe' result needs to be compared against the regional baseline, not just the federal MCLs.

Ken collects samples on-site during the home inspection (or in a separate visit), following the chain-of-custody protocols required by Massachusetts state-certified labs. Samples are delivered to the lab the same day.

items.q: What if results come back outside acceptable levels?
items.a: Most contaminants have well-established treatment options: bacteria can be addressed with chlorination and UV disinfection; iron and manganese with media filters; arsenic with reverse osmosis or specific arsenic media; high sodium often points to road-salt infiltration that needs further investigation. We discuss results and refer you to qualified treatment installers.

items.q: Is well water testing required for FHA or VA loans?
items.a: Yes — and the required panel is more comprehensive than a homeowner's basic test. Ken's pre-purchase well water panel meets or exceeds FHA, VA, and most conventional lender requirements. Confirm with your loan officer to make sure the right tests are ordered.

cards.title: Buyer Home Inspection in Amherst

cards.title: Pre-Listing Home Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Find out what a buyer's inspector will find — before you list. Get ahead of objections, price the home accurately, and shorten your time on market.

cards.title: New Construction Home Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: An independent inspection before your warranty closes. Phase, pre-drywall, or final-walk inspections — finding what the builder's punch list misses.

cards.title: Condo & Townhouse Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Targeted inspection of your unit plus the shared-system review most condo buyers don't get — common to mill conversions, downtown buildings, and townhouse complexes across Western Mass.

cards.title: Radon Testing in Amherst
cards.body: 48-hour continuous-monitor radon testing in the Pioneer Valley. Add to any home inspection or schedule standalone.

cards.title: Septic System Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Pre-purchase septic system evaluation for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Coordinated with Massachusetts Title 5 inspectors when transfer requires it.

cards.title: Termite & WDI Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: NPMA-33 wood-destroying insect inspection — required for most VA, FHA, and many investor loans. Carpenter ant, carpenter bee, and powderpost beetle evaluation included.

cards.title: Mold Inspection in Amherst
cards.body: Visual mold evaluation with moisture-source identification, optional air sampling. Common in older Pioneer Valley basements and mill-conversion units.
